composition.Programmer 1:
Implement backend code in app.py
- Develop the main backend Python application using Flask.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard).
- Implement routing for all frontend pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend code.
- Implement business logic for user authentication (if any), book browsing, borrowing, returning, reservations, reviews, profile management, and fine payments.
- Implement file read/write operations for all data files in the 'data' directory, using plain text and starting from the first line without encryption.
- Frontend code files to be supported by backend routes  dashboard.html, book_catalog.html, book_details.html, borrow_confirmation.html, my_borrowings.html, my_reservations.html, my_reviews.html, write_review.html, user_profile.html, payment_confirmation.html.
- Incorporate the original task's supplementary conditions and data formats.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop frontend templates in the templates/ directory
- Complete all HTML template files corresponding to the ten pages specified.
- Use placeholders and template syntax to dynamically display data passed from backend.
- Ensure all URLs in the frontend code are routes defined in the backend, not direct HTML file names.
- Templates to implement  dashboard.html, book_catalog.html, book_details.html, borrow_confirmation.html, my_borrowings.html, my_reservations.html, my_reviews.html, write_review.html, user_profile.html, payment_confirmation.html.
- Follow the detailed page design and element IDs as specified in the requirements document.
composition.Programmer 3:
Develop static assets in the static/ directory
- Implement CSS stylesheets for consistent UI styling across all pages (css/styles.css).
- Implement JavaScript files for client-side interactivity, including button actions and form validation (js/scripts.js).
- Organize and add book cover images and other graphical assets in static/images/.
- Ensure static assets support the frontend templates and enhance user experience.
